WITN01020200
WITN01020200
DocuSign Envelope ID: D94327B5-F47E-49D0-B89C-527DDE221AEB

Paragraph 4.23: In or around March 2015, the POL board sub-committee decided not to give Second Sight access to POL's internal prosecution files that were subject to legal professional privilege.

59. As at 24 June 2020, I believed it to be true that in or around March 2015, the POL board sub-committee decided not to give Second Sight access to POL's internal prosecution files that were subject to legal professional privilege. I am no longer able to recall why, in June 2020, I believed that it was the POL board sub-committee which made this decision.

Paragraph 4.24: You had previously raised the issue of remote access with the then CEO of Fujitsu, who said the Horizon IT System was "like Fort Knox".

60. As at 24 June 2020, I believed it to be true that I had previously raised the issue of remote access with the then CEO of Fujitsu, who had said that the Horizon IT System was "like Fort Knox": see paragraphs 1299 and 1796 of my witness statement dated 8 March 2024 (WITN01020100).

Paragraph 4.25: When you were CEO of POL, you were told by Fujitsu that it was not possible for branch records to be altered remotely without SPMs' knowledge.

Paragraph 4.26: When you were CEO of POL, you believed that it was not possible for branch records to be altered remotely without SPMs' knowledge.

Page 20 of 27
